title: coreopsis jardinii
intro: Coreopsis jardinii, also known as Mexican tickseed, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Coreopsis jardinii include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues. Coreopsis jardinii is often prepared as a tea or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, and it can also be used topically as an infusion or poultice to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
